sections_cited: Section 4 of the Sindh Service Tribunal Act

chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR C. P. No. D – 219 of 2023 Date of hearing Order with signature of Judge Hearing of case 1. For orders on office objection at Flag-A 2. For hearing of main case 01.06.2023 Mr. Iftikhar Ali Arain, Advocate for the petitioner. Mr. Mehboob Ali Wassan, Assistant Advocate General Sindh..-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.- The petitioner is admittedly a Civil Servant, who through instant petition seeks directions to respondents No.1 to 3 to promote him and to give all the back benefits by deciding his appeals, which are pending before the respondent No.1 including last one dated 05.12.2022. Section 4 of the Sindh Service Tribunal Act, 1973 provides adequate remedy to a Civil Servant for redressal of his such grievances by approaching to Tribunals; even in case the appeal or representation is not decided by the departmental authority within ninety days of the appeal or representation, the aggrieved Civil Servant may approach to Service Tribunal for the redressal of his grievances. However, in view of bar contained under Article 212 of the Constitution of the Islamic Republic of Pakistan, 1973, instant petition filed under Article 199 of the Constitution is not maintainable in law; hence, the same dismissed, leaving the petitioner at liberty to approach the relevant forum for redressal of his grievances. J U D G E J U D G E Abdul Basit
